title: Decision + hardening: shared-infra devcontainer baseline ownership (Docker contents, Makefile flow, runtime guarantees)
description:
## Summary
Create a decision and implementation task based on website-agent validation feedback to determine how prescriptive shared-infra should be for target repo Docker/devcontainer build contents, then harden the baseline accordingly.

## Key Decision Question
Should shared-infra explicitly dictate required runtime/tooling in target repo Docker build (for example Node 22 + npm), or keep Docker content minimally opinionated and push runtime responsibility to each target project?

## Feedback Inputs (from website-agent report)
- Runtime requirement mismatch risk: README expects Node 22/npm 10+, but target Dockerfile may not install Node/npm.
- Command mismatch risk: AGENTS workflow references make commands that may fail if repo only has Makefile.workteam and no root Makefile shim.
- Recursive make risk in Makefile.workteam agent-runtime-resolve path (missing explicit -f file propagation).
- Devcontainer mount portability risk from HOME/USERPROFILE concatenation.

## Scope
1. Record explicit architectural decision for shared-infra baseline strictness (dictated runtime vs minimal baseline).
2. Define/update acceptance contract for target repos (required vs optional runtime tooling).
3. If strict baseline is chosen, implement and validate Docker/devcontainer updates for deterministic runtime.
4. Align AGENTS/README command guidance with Makefile.workteam usage expectations.
5. Fix make recursion and mount portability guidance/template behavior where applicable.
6. Add/adjust verification checks to catch drift early.

## Deliverables
- Decision documented (tradeoffs, rationale, chosen policy).
- Updated templates/docs/scripts reflecting chosen policy.
- Validation evidence from bootstrap + container rebuild + in-container checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear documented answer to Docker ownership question.
- New target repo bootstrap yields a usable agent runtime consistent with documented requirements.
- Commands in docs are executable as written in target repos.
- Devcontainer/mount behavior is robust across host OS variants.
notes:
Implemented requested Docker/runtime alignment fixes for target repo bootstrap templates.

Changes
- Updated templates/.devcontainer/Dockerfile.template to use Node 22 base image and ensure Python tooling is installed.
- Clarified README to distinguish:
  - root Dockerfile used by shared-infra repository itself
  - .devcontainer/Dockerfile used by bootstrapped target repositories
- Documented that target baseline includes Node 22 + Python3 packages.

Validation
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-layout
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-skill
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-templates

Result
- Validation passed; template/runtime contract now explicitly includes both Node and Python for target repo container builds.

title: Define pre-devcontainer bootstrap path and document container prerequisites
description:
## Summary
Ensure shared-infra onboarding explicitly supports repositories that do not yet have devcontainer/docker scaffolding, by defining a pre-devcontainer bootstrap sequence and documenting it end-to-end.

## Problem
Current process can be interpreted as requiring target repo container assets before bootstrap. For new repos, this is a blocker because Docker/devcontainer files are part of what shared-infra should provision.

## Scope
- Define required sequence for first-time setup before any devcontainer build.
- Ensure bootstrap path can run from host shell and lay down required container baseline files.
- Validate that post-bootstrap the target repo has sufficient assets to build/reopen in container.
- Update README with explicit "pre-devcontainer" flow and validation checklist.
- Include expected Beads UI readiness path after bootstrap + container open.

## Acceptance Criteria
- README has a clearly labeled pre-devcontainer bootstrap section.
- Steps explicitly indicate what runs before vs after container build.
- Validation commands confirm target repo has required devcontainer and agent baseline files.
- Process includes Beads UI readiness guidance for first session in target repo.
notes:
Completed implementation for pre-devcontainer bootstrap path and docs updates.

Changes made
- Added pre-devcontainer requirements into bootstrap process by provisioning:
  - .devcontainer/devcontainer.json
  - .devcontainer/Dockerfile
  - .devcontainer/postCreate.sh
- Added new template: templates/.devcontainer/Dockerfile.template
- Updated templates/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json.template to point at .devcontainer/Dockerfile
- Updated scripts/shared_infra_sync.sh to sync .devcontainer/Dockerfile into target repos
- Updated scripts/verify_target_agent_setup.sh to require .devcontainer/Dockerfile
- Updated Makefile.shared verify-templates to assert Dockerfile.template exists
- Updated README with explicit "Pre-devcontainer bootstrap (critical first-time flow)" section, including before-vs-after container steps and Beads UI readiness guidance

Validation executed
- make -f Makefile.shared target-bootstrap TARGET_ROOT=.tmp-target-repo SHARED_ROOT=.
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-target-agent-setup TARGET_ROOT=.tmp-target-repo
- test -f .tmp-target-repo/.devcontainer/Dockerfile
- make -f Makefile.shared target-refresh TARGET_ROOT=.tmp-target-repo SHARED_ROOT=.
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-layout
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-skill
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-templates

Result
- All validations passed.

title: Design and implement shared-infra bootstrap and refresh workflow for new projects
description:
## Summary
Design and build the shared-infra onboarding and refresh mechanism so a new repository can import common agent infrastructure with one command, and later sync updates as new shared skills/plugins are added.

## Problem
Current setup is not yet a fully packaged, repeatable workflow for greenfield project bootstrap plus safe refresh over time.

## Scope
- Define bootstrap command/script for first-time project setup from shared-infra.
- Define refresh command/script to pull new common skills/plugins and agent setup updates.
- Include agent setup initialization flow in bootstrap/refresh design.
- Define conflict-handling policy for local overlays vs shared baseline.
- Add/update validation checks so projects can verify install/refresh correctness.
- Document usage contract and rollout/versioning expectations.

## Deliverables
- Implemented bootstrap entrypoint.
- Implemented refresh entrypoint.
- Agent setup integration included.
- Validation command(s) and acceptance checks.
- Updated docs with operator instructions and troubleshooting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New project can run a single bootstrap command and become agent-ready.
- Existing project can run refresh and receive new shared skills/plugins without destructive overwrite.
- Agent setup assets are provisioned and validated as part of workflow.
- QA checklist exists and is executable for both bootstrap and refresh paths.
- Documentation clearly describes first-time setup, refresh cadence, and rollback/recovery.
notes:
Progress update: bootstrap + refresh implementation started and validated.

Implemented
- Added Make targets in Makefile.shared:
  - target-bootstrap
  - target-refresh
  - verify-target-agent-setup
- Added scripts:
  - scripts/shared_infra_sync.sh
  - scripts/verify_target_agent_setup.sh
- Added README usage documentation for one-command bootstrap/refresh and target verification.

Behavior
- bootstrap mode: non-destructive (creates missing files only)
- refresh mode: syncs shared-managed assets and writes backup files before replacement
- writes lock metadata to .agent/shared-infra.lock in target repo
- sync includes shared skills, profiles, GitHub skill entrypoints, AGENTS/Makefile/devcontainer/VSCode baseline templates

Validation run
- make -f Makefile.shared target-bootstrap TARGET_ROOT=.tmp-target-repo SHARED_ROOT=.
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-target-agent-setup TARGET_ROOT=.tmp-target-repo
- make -f Makefile.shared target-refresh TARGET_ROOT=.tmp-target-repo SHARED_ROOT=.
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-layout
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-skill
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-templates

Result: all checks passed.

Open follow-up
- Exclude transient artifacts from sync path (e.g., any accidental generated files in skill trees) via policy and test hardening.

title: QA: Validate shared-infra agent setup against docs/agent_setup_spec.md
description:
## Summary
Run a QA validation of the shared baseline repository against the agent setup contract documented in docs/agent_setup_spec.md.

## Scope
- Validate runtime contract commands and environment behavior.
- Validate minimal file contract artifacts are present.
- Validate shared docs and skill directories expected by the spec.
- Validate verification command contract in the spec versus actual Make targets.

## Findings to capture
- PASS/NITS/FAIL status with concrete command/file evidence.
- Any contract drift between docs and implementation.
- Recommended remediation tasks for drift.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Child task is linked under epic shared-infra-ad1.
- Validation evidence is recorded in issue notes.
- Contract drift (if any) is explicitly documented with severity.
- Follow-up action is defined for any FAIL/NITS items.
notes:
QA Validation Report: shared-infra baseline vs docs/agent_setup_spec.md
Timestamp: 2026-04-27T01:03Z

Overall verdict: NITS (repo is largely configured correctly; one spec/implementation drift found)

Evidence

1) Runtime detection/init contract — PASS
- Command: make -f Makefile.shared agent-runtime-resolve
- Result: resolved container-beads, runtime check passed.
- Additional environment evidence from make -f Makefile.shared agent-repro:
  - node=v22.16.0
  - npm=10.9.2
  - make=GNU Make 4.3
  - git=2.49.0
  - bd CLI available

2) Minimal file contract presence — PASS
Validated present:
- AGENTS.md
- Makefile.shared
- .agent/profiles/container-beads.md
- .agent/profiles/local-lite.md
- .devcontainer/devcontainer.json
- .devcontainer/postCreate.sh
- .agents/skills/ (directory)
- .github/skills/ (directory)

3) Shared docs expected by spec — PASS
- docs/agent_setup_spec.md (source contract)
- docs/skills_and_components_classification.md
- docs/beads_troubleshooting.md

4) Validation command contract in spec — NITS (doc drift)
Spec currently states:
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-layout
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-skill
- make -f Makefile.shared verify-templates

Actual state:
- These targets do not exist in Makefile.shared (make fails: "No rule to make target 'verify-layout'").

Severity
- NITS (documentation drift, not a runtime blocker for current repo operation)

Recommendation
- Align docs/agent_setup_spec.md Validation Contract section with implemented targets (e.g., agent-runtime-resolve, agent-repro, agent-qa-check, plus concrete file contract checks), OR
- Add verify-layout/verify-skill/verify-templates targets to Makefile.shared and keep current doc wording.

Next action
- Create follow-up task to reconcile spec validation section with actual Make targets, then close after doc/Make parity is restored.

title: Set up the Beads UI
description:
## Summary
Set up and validate the Beads UI experience for this repository so issues can be inspected and managed visually.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Beads UI can be launched from this repo
- Core view (issue list/details) renders successfully
- Basic usage steps are documented in issue notes/comments

title: General Continuous Improvement
description:
## Summary
Establish an ongoing epic to track broad, incremental improvements across shared infrastructure, developer workflow, and automation quality.

## Outcomes
- Maintain a durable container for continuous improvement tasks
- Keep improvements discoverable and prioritized
- Enable child tasks to be linked and tracked under a single strategic umbrella
